summary: Session 2 shipped the release-side git-workflow automation: three confirm-gated, AI-invokable VS Code commands, ALL PURE GIT (host-agnostic by construction, so no host adapter/CLI preflight this session), each a thin auditable wrapper that prints the exact command lines before running. New src/commands/gitRelease.ts reuses Session 1's injectable process/UI seam (imports ProcessRunner/RunResult/GitWorkflowUi/defaultRunner/defaultUi/detectTrunkBranch from gitWorkflow.ts; added a one-word export to defaultUi). 'Dabbler: Cut release tag' = annotated tag create + `git push origin <tag>` behind a MANDATORY, NON-BYPASSABLE operator confirm (the release gate; the tag is pinned to the resolved commit sha, not the mutable ref, so it tags exactly the reviewed commit; refuses when the tag already exists=immutable, when the ref is unresolvable, or when there is no origin; on push-fail keeps the local tag and gives retry/delete recovery). 'Dabbler: Start hotfix from tag' = `git switch -c hotfix/<name> <tag>` (refuses a dirty tree so the branch is exactly the tagged snapshot; refuses an existing branch). 'Dabbler: Roll back to tag' = `git checkout <tag>` detached-HEAD redeploy (refuses a dirty tree; names the return-to-trunk command). Pure helpers refNameProblem (git check-ref-format subset, tags+branches) and parseTagRefLines. These encapsulate the EXACT Part-10 drills of docs/tutorials/module-team-hello-world.md so Session 3 can re-cut the tutorial to reference them by name. Wired extension.ts + package.json (3 commands); updated the watcherInventory allowlist line numbers (208->209, 244->245; the gitRelease import shifted both callsites +1). Tests: 54 new unit tests (src/test/suite/gitRelease.test.ts) over injected seams — the mandatory-confirm gate + confirm-declined (non-bypassable), tag-format validation table, tag-exists/unresolved-ref/no-origin/push-failed-after-local-create surfaces, sha-pinning, hotfix dirty-refuse/branch-exists/invalid-name/git-error, rollback dirty-refuse/checkout-fail/git-error, + a package.json command-surface pin — and a real-git dogfood (src/test/dogfood/gitRelease.dogfood.ts, outside the CI glob) proving all four drills against a scratch repo + local bare remote: annotated tag created AND pushed; hotfix branch cut from the TAGGED commit (not main's advanced tip); rollback lands a detached HEAD at the tagged commit; immutability guard refuses re-cutting an existing tag. Suites: extension unit 1767 passing (1713 baseline + 54), 0 failing (via the shipped vscode-stub mocha path; the @vscode/test-electron launcher is known-broken on VS Code 1.128 and is not a regression); pytest Layer 1 unchanged at 3030 passed / 6 skipped (no Python touched); tsc + eslint clean. Layer 3 Playwright deliberately NOT run: L-064-12 scopes it to Explorer-rendering/state-writer/fixture changes, and this session touches none (command files + registration + package.json only). Verification: discovery round 1 (gpt-5-6, cross-provider, anthropic auto-excluded, fan-out 2/2) returned VERIFIED with ZERO blocking findings and six Minor nits; adjudicated per the L-095-1 consequence rubric — TWO fixed (commit-sha pinning across the confirm boundary; listTags now returns null on git failure vs [] for 'no tags', so hotfix/rollback report a real error from a non-repo folder instead of a misleading 'no tags'), each covered by new deterministic unit tests + the dogfood re-run; FOUR accepted/dismissed with reasons (shell-safe display is advisory since execution is shell-free; hotfix/ prefix is a deliberate default not an invariant; branch/tag same-name push ambiguity fails loud and is recoverable; 'PR' is host-neutral across the two supported hosts, GitLab being an explicit non-goal). No re-verify round was opened (Minor-only stop; the fixes are strict improvements proven by falsifiers). Total verification cost ~$0.40. No version bump or changelog this session by design — those and the tutorial re-cut are Session 3's deliverables (the release boundary is after the set closes; publish is operator-gated).

reason.specifics: Session 3 (the last session) is documentation- and critique-heavy: re-cut the hello-world tutorial to an automation-first, host-neutral main flow, move raw git/host-CLI sequences into a reference appendix, write the per-host (Azure DevOps + GitHub/GHE) setup section to the operator quality bar, retake screenshots, sweep docs, update both changelogs, and run the advisory path-aware critique + Step 9. The routed step-3.5 analysis (s2-ai-assignment-analysis.json) recommends a frontier-tier model for that higher-order authoring/reasoning; the current orchestrator (claude-opus-4-8) already fits, so continuity is warranted rather than a switch. gpt/openai gpt-5-6 is an equivalent provider-diversity alternative for the prose/critique work.
